source_refs.rationale: Only explicit customer-visible collaboration events may fan out into optional customer email.
Notification routing, continuity, and customer-safe projection must remain explicit and typed.
Contextual help acknowledgements must preserve route and case context instead of becoming free-form tickets.
Current provider documentation exposes API-managed template aliases and server-scoped template records.
Current provider webhook posture is configured per server and authenticated with HTTPS, Basic Auth, and custom headers.

truth_boundary_statement: External email delivery events remain transport or observability projections only. They may append internal notification evidence, suppression posture, retry posture, and counters, but they never mutate work-item truth, approval truth, portal-help lifecycle truth, or authority truth directly.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Notification ID
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Stable correlation key for internal delivery evidence and dedupe.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Item ID
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Opaque object correlation ID; never rendered alone to the customer.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Item title
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Customer-safe title from the aligned projection.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Period label
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Customer-safe period or filing range label.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Reference label
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Plain-language case or request reference suitable for the portal.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Customer status
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Literal customer-safe state label; no internal gate or escalation terms.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Request info ref
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Opaque request-for-info reference used for focus routing and dedupe.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Request info summary
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Customer-safe summary of what is needed next.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Request info due date
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Plain-language due date label when policy allows it to be shown.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Staff comment preview
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Short customer-visible preview of the latest shared comment.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Customer due date
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Customer-safe due date string used when due date changes are notifyable.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Resolution summary
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Plain-language completion summary with no internal cause coding.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Help request ID
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Opaque contextual-help artifact reference.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Help reason
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Literal help-reason label carried from the request context.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Support channel
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Bounded support channel label for acknowledgement copy.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Case context summary
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Short context-preserving summary so the customer need not restate the case.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Detail route
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Exact lawful in-app destination route for follow-through.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Focus anchor
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Exact customer-safe focus anchor for continuity.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Return route
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Lawful parent return route when the notification is opened.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Return focus anchor
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Parent return focus anchor carried by continuity contracts.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Fallback route
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Safe browser fallback route when the original detail route cannot reopen.

safe_merge_variable_definitions.label: Fallback focus anchor
safe_merge_variable_definitions.description: Focus target paired with the safe fallback route.

template_records.label: New request for information
template_records.preheader_template: A new request for information is ready in your Taxat portal.
template_records.headline_template: We need a little more information
body_template_paragraphs: We've added a request for information to your case for {{ item_title }}.
Please review {{ request_info_summary }} and reply in the portal by {{ request_info_due_date_label }}.
footer_template_paragraphs: Need support? Use Help in the portal and we'll keep the same case context.
fallback_rules: If any required merge variable is missing, fail configuration and do not queue send.
Do not substitute internal notes, gate language, or assignment labels when request-for-info summary is missing.

sample_render_model.request_info_summary: Please confirm your freelance income and upload the missing invoice.

source_refs.rationale: New request-for-info is a notifyable customer event family.

notes: Customer-safe projection fields only; no internal actor or gate context.

template_records.label: New staff comment shared with customer
template_records.preheader_template: A Taxat reviewer shared a customer-visible update in the portal.
template_records.headline_template: There is a new update on your case
body_template_paragraphs: A Taxat reviewer has shared a customer-visible comment on {{ item_title }}.
footer_template_paragraphs: Use Help in the portal if you need support. We'll keep the same case context.
fallback_rules: If the comment preview is missing, fail configuration rather than falling back to internal thread content.

sample_render_model.staff_comment_preview: We've checked the first upload and left one follow-up note in the portal.

source_refs.rationale: Shared staff comments are explicitly notifyable to customers.

notes: Do not include individual staff identity when tenant policy masks it.

template_records.label: Customer due date changed
template_records.preheader_template: The next due date in your Taxat portal has been created or updated.
body_template_paragraphs: The next due date for {{ item_title }} has been set or updated in the portal.
footer_template_paragraphs: If you need help with this request, use Help in the portal so the same case context stays attached.
fallback_rules: If no customer-safe due date label is available, block the template instead of inferring one from internal SLA or escalation state.

source_refs.rationale: Customer due date creation or change is a notifyable family when the source contract allows it.

notes: Do not turn overdue or escalation-only state into outbound copy.

template_records.label: Item resolved or closed
template_records.preheader_template: A request in your Taxat portal is resolved or closed.
body_template_paragraphs: We've marked {{ item_title }} as resolved or closed in the portal.
footer_template_paragraphs: You can still open the portal to review the completed item and its final summary.
fallback_rules: If no customer-safe resolution summary exists, block the email instead of translating internal state transitions into generic reassurance.

sample_render_model.resolution_summary: This request is complete and no further action is needed right now.

source_refs.rationale: Item resolved or closed is a notifyable customer event family.

notes: Completion copy must stay literal and avoid implying authority-of-record settlement.

template_records.label: Portal help acknowledged
template_records.subject_template: We received your help request about {{ item_title }}
template_records.preheader_template: Your help request was saved with the same case context in the Taxat portal.
body_template_paragraphs: We received your help request and kept the same case context for {{ item_title }}.
footer_template_paragraphs: You don't need to restate the case. We'll continue from the same request context.
fallback_rules: If help-request identity or help reason is missing, block the template and keep the acknowledgement in-app only.

sample_render_model.help_reason_label: Question about the upload you requested
sample_render_model.case_context_summary: Your help request is linked to the same filing request and upload panel.

source_refs.rationale: Portal help allocation is a durable contextual-support event.
Help acknowledgements must preserve route and case context rather than forcing blank restatement.

notes: This is product-owned acknowledgement mail, not generic provider support mail.

template_records.label: Support contact acknowledged
template_records.preheader_template: Your support contact was recorded with the same case context in Taxat.
body_template_paragraphs: We logged your support request through the {{ support_channel_label }} channel for {{ item_title }}.
footer_template_paragraphs: We kept the same route and focus context so you do not need to restate the case.
fallback_rules: If the support channel or case-context summary is missing, keep the acknowledgement inside the portal and raise a typed configuration error.

sample_render_model.case_context_summary: We kept the same request and focus context with your support message.

source_refs.rationale: Support-contact acknowledgements must preserve contextual case routing.

notes: This acknowledgement is customer-safe and channel-bounded.

typed_gaps: shared_operating_contract_0038_to_0045.md was absent at execution time, so this pack grounded itself directly in the algorithm corpus, pc_0041, pc_0031, pc_0033, and current provider documentation.
Open and click tracking remain disabled by default until a later explicit product/privacy decision justifies them.
notes: Only customer-visible collaboration and contextual-help events are modeled here.
Provider-owned identity, password-reset, or MFA mail remains outside this catalog and outside product workflow truth.
